cross_referenced_hnote: HNOTE-PART-8B-FAIR-AUDITOR-001 (deliberately deferred to next analyst run — one item per run; EXP-183 cross-links it)
recommended_actions: (1) Extend data/wins.json schema with author_claim_class, author_registry_status, author_strict_prospective_confirmed, author_visible_badge, author_registry_integrity_note, author_rating_last_checked fields. See EXP-183.schema_extension_proposal for exact field specs, source files, rendering defaults, and backfill plan.

(2) Edit build-scripts/generate-html.js to render an "Author's Self-Rating" block on each WIN detail card, placed between the existing "Our verdict" block and the detailed prose. Muted secondary styling; collapsed stub for WINs with all-null author_* fields (avoids visual nagging on 60 unclassified WINs).

(3) Analyst Mode 2 next run: backfill author_* fields for 12 WINs (the 9 claims classified in claim_index.json + WIN-058/058b/062 integrity notes). Set author_rating_last_checked = 2026-04-18. Remaining 57 WINs keep null defaults.

(4) Add a new entry to data/sections.json with tab: "part4" and id: "registry-framing-audit" containing the replacement_html in EXP-183 (the "4.2 Registry Framing Audit" subsection covering the 6 registry-level aggregate gaps).

(5) Edit build-scripts/generate-html.js Timestamp Error tab (approximately lines 1520–1640) to insert the timestampTabCalloutHtml paragraph near the methodology references. DO NOT change the tab id, label, or OTS-focused content — this is a 2–3 sentence cross-link only.

NEW ISSUE: Open ISS-NEXT to track the newly-discovered prospective_confirmed count drift (ai_manifest.json=10, visible grid=9, strict_prospective_confirmed in claim_index.json=1). Not previously tracked.

POLLER HOOK: Add a daily diff of predictions/claim_index.json.entries[] against wins.json author_* fields. Any new author classification triggers an attention-inbox item for the analyst to populate the row. If classified_count grows toward 69 over time, the taxonomy is a real audit in progress; if it stalls near 9, the taxonomy is a definition that has not been applied.

SELF-AUDIT: Add to integrity agent checklist — scan our own prose for hardcoded counts that should be computed from source files. EXP-183 explicitly invokes the "never hardcode a count that can be derived" rule; we must apply it to ourselves.

DO NOT CLOSE on EXP-183 alone: ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1089, ISS-1117, ISS-1074. EXP-183 catalogs the pattern and proposes the data model; the individual issues still require their resolution paths. Specifically ISS-1117 (WIN-058/062 collisions) is largely subsumed by the schema extension once backfilled — consider closing after backfill completes.
summary: EXP-183 REVISED 2026-04-18 per user feedback. Original single-tab placement rejected — overloaded Timestamp Error scope. Revised placement is three-part coordinated: (E) extend data/wins.json schema with six author_* fields to host per-WIN author self-rating; render an "Author's Self-Rating" block on each WIN detail card alongside "Our verdict" — side-by-side visibility for per-WIN gaps. (F) Add new Part 4.2 "Registry Framing Audit" subsection to host six registry-level aggregate gaps that don't map to individual WINs: 94.5%/69-confirmed headline vs strict_prospective_confirmed=1; 5-class taxonomy applied to 9/69 claims; newly-discovered prospective_confirmed count drift 10/9/1; live.html 67-confirmed footer; registry_semantics="display-level bookkeeping" framing; CVaR exclusion_cap=6 not at point-of-emphasis. (G) Short 2–3 sentence taxonomy callout+link in Timestamp Error tab — references methodology.json taxonomy and cross-links to 4.2 and to the WIN schema extension, without overloading OTS scope. Natural-expansion property: future author classifications land in existing wins.json slots; audit shrinks as author reconciles visible/API layers.
newsworthy: Newly-discovered: three different layers of the dome site disagree on the author's own headline metric. ai_manifest.json counts prospective_confirmed=10, the visible grid shows 9, and claim_index.json strict_prospective_confirmed counts 1. Plus: the methodology document's 5-class claim taxonomy has been applied to 9 of 69+ claims — rigor is a definition, not yet an audit. Our site now encodes author self-ratings as first-class data so every future classification lands automatically.
other_side_considered: EXP-183 includes a counterpoint_considered section listing 7 concerns (machine-readable legitimacy, iteration-in-progress, academic-paper analogy, edit-budget fairness, selection bias in the 9, observer effect if we surface this, strawman risk if the author disclaims the visible layer entirely). Each has an in-line mitigation in the replacement_html — the catalog is framed as "these concessions exist; the headline framing has not caught up" rather than "the author is hiding things".

mark_hnote_consumed.consumed_by: EXP-183 — structural framing catalog with 4 placement options (B primary, A secondary) + 9-row evidence table

superseded_recommendation.note: The original recommendation (Option B primary: generalize Timestamp Error tab) was rejected by user feedback 2026-04-18. Preserved in EXP-183.placement_options with status=REJECTED_BY_USER_FEEDBACK_2026-04-18 for audit trail.
superseded_recommendation.reason: Overloaded the OTS canary tab scope. User feedback verbatim: "so i think we overloaded our new data model by putting it in time stamp. specifically the taxonomy component might merit a reference/call out/link in time stamp, but the substance of that should probably be on our wins detail tab so that we show how we're rating vs how the author says he's rating. ultimately, we should probably also expand the fields in our detail tab to provide transparancy of the authors rating so if he fills in more they have natural places to go."

revision_notes.summary: User feedback reframed placement from single-tab (Option B) to three-part: schema extension + Part 4.2 subsection + Timestamp callout-only. Per-WIN substance moves to WIN detail cards via new author_* fields. Registry-level aggregates move to new Part 4.2 subsection. Taxonomy callout preserved in Timestamp Error tab as a cross-link only.
